findings.finding: Authorization did not require current account, membership and tenant state.
findings.impact: Revoked or suspended principals could retain access until session expiry.
findings.fix: Authorization now requires live state objects on every request and denies non-active states.

findings.finding: A session with an issue time in the future was accepted.
findings.impact: Invalid or clock-manipulated session claims could be used before validity.
findings.fix: Authorization rejects current time earlier than issued_at.

findings.finding: Session lifetime had no upper bound.
findings.impact: An excessively long-lived session could expand the revocation exposure window.

findings.finding: Credential and recovery verifier references could be identical.
findings.impact: Credential and recovery controls could unintentionally share one verifier record.
findings.fix: PseudonymousAccount rejects reused credential and recovery references.

observed_defects: credential and recovery reference reuse was accepted
session contract lacked live-state and TTL requirements
